event: radical cystoprostatectomy and ileal neobladder reconstruction (Studer Pouch)
post-operative recovery complicated by persistent urinary incontinence
high pressures (maximum functional capacity 270 ml, pressure at maximum capacity 35 cm H20)
increased pouch capacity to approximately 550 ml
presented to emergency department 8 weeks post operatively
treated for urinary tract infection with oral antibiotics
arterial blood gas showing uncompensated metabolic acidosis
initial diagnosis of severe urosepsis with acute kidney injury
repeat arterial blood gas showing worsening hyperchloremic metabolic acidosis
identification of hyperchloremic metabolic acidosis as reversible complication
improving metabolic acidosis on repeat ABGs
neobladder excision and ileal conduit formation
no further episodes of metabolic acidosis
